examples.title: Thompson's Construction: Regex to NFA
examples.description: Algorithm to convert regular expression to NFA with epsilon transitions
examples.code:
ALGORITHM Thompson(regex)
BEGIN
 1. Parse regex into postfix notation (add concatenation operators)
 2. Initialize empty stack for NFA fragments
 3. FOR EACH token in postfix expression:
 a. IF literal: push NFA for single character
 b. IF |: pop two fragments, build union NFA
 c. IF *: pop one fragment, build Kleene star NFA
 d. IF ⋅: pop two fragments, build concatenation NFA
 4. Final NFA = popped fragment with start and accept states
END
examples.explanation:
Step 3a: Single character NFA has two states with character transition
Step 3b: Union creates new start/accept states with ε-transitions
Step 3c: Kleene star adds new ε-transitions for looping
Step 3d: Concatenation links accept of first to start of second

examples.title: NFA Simulation for Regex Matching
examples.description: Backtracking approach to test string acceptance by regex-derived NFA
examples.code:
FUNCTION Match(nfa, input_str)
BEGIN
 1. current_states = ε_closure(nfa.start)
 2. FOR EACH char IN input_str:
 a. next_states = ε_closure(move(current_states, char))
 b. IF next_states empty: RETURN False
 c. current_states = next_states
 3. RETURN any state in current_states is accept state
END

FUNCTION ε_closure(states)
BEGIN
 RETURN all states reachable via ε-transitions from input states
END
examples.explanation:
Line 1: Initialize with start state's ε-closure
Line 2a: Compute transitions for current character and their ε-closures
Line 3: Check if any final state is accepting

examples.title: Regex Validation using Finite Automata
examples.description: DFA implementation for pattern a+b* (demonstrates regex → DFA conversion)
examples.explanation:
Transition table implements DFA for regex a+b*
State 0: initial
State 1: 'a' seen (accepting)
State 2: 'b' seen (accepting)
State 3: 'a' followed by 'b's (accepting)

examples.title: Brzozowski's Minimization Algorithm
examples.description: Algorithm to minimize DFA using reversal and determinization
examples.code:
ALGORITHM MinimizeDFA(dfa)
BEGIN
 1. Reverse all transitions in DFA
 2. Convert reversed NFA to DFA (powerset construction)
 3. Repeat steps 1-2 with new DFA
 4. Resulting DFA is minimal equivalent
END
examples.explanation:
Step 1: Creates NFA with reversed transitions
Step 2: Determinization removes unreachable states
Repeating the process yields minimal DFA
